parentQuestion: Which membrane lines body cavities that open to the exterior?

scaffolds.explain: The cutaneous membrane is your skin — the largest organ in your body. It's a tough, keratinized, waterproof barrier covering your entire external surface. Without it, you'd dry out like a raisin in minutes. Dramatic? Yes. True? Absolutely.

scaffolds.q: Does the digestive tract (mouth to anus) have an opening to the exterior?
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The digestive tract is technically a continuous tube THROUGH your body, opening to the exterior at both the mouth and anus. Topologically speaking, you're a very complicated donut. This means anything inside your digestive tract is technically still 'outside' your body — it hasn't crossed the membrane barrier into your internal tissues yet.
